title: thymus serpyllum subsp. parviflorus
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. parviflorus, commonly known as wild thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is a low-growing, flowering perennial herb with fragrant leaves and stems, native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of wild thyme include its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiviral properties, which make it effective in treating respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin infections. Wild thyme can be consumed as a tea, used in infusions, or taken as a supplement in capsule or tincture form, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cognitive function, and supporting immune system function.
